text: User: A session-scoped Stop hook is now active with condition: "make it real, real features, real functionalities, use openrouter/ollama models via rig.rs, just like ~/Work/win app does. Allow configuring the providers/model for the agent and tools -- Provide a real settings and rich settings and other screens that would be necessary. Use an opus agent to determine when we have the real features ready to meet what the product needs, including agent memories, tools, screens, interface to send messages to the coach. Use PRs + merge code back to master. use sonnet agents for coding, haiku agents to test on the device. Deploy to my iphone when you are done. Ensure the app ends up polished and sleek, with everything a finished app needs. I want to be able to immediately sync my workouts to iCloud or, even better, as a private github repo that the app commits to constantly (wouldn't that be cool?!) like the user would signup with github, a new repo is created with their workouts and the app commits a new markdown file with the changes of each workday -- frequently the app pulls from github to sync up any changes the user might have done such that changes done outside the app can be ingested by the app, and reviewed by the agent (like the agent might see, oh, we have new commits, let's see if there's something I should take into account) [...] Assistant: Key findings: toolchain is ready (Rust iOS targets + `uniffi-bindgen` installed), `gh` is authed as **pablof7z** (repo scope), **WorkoutMD is already a git repo on `master` with an `origin`**, the user wired **code signing** (`DEVELOPMENT_TEAM C99QRJCR43`, automatic) into `project.yml`, and **Pablo's iPhone 17 Pro Max is connected** for deploy. `~/src/tenex-edge` is a Rust nostr/NIP-29 project (good — that agent is mapping it).
